question.content: COF-1 is formed by condensation of diboronic acid building blocks into a periodic covalent framework. Based on the illustrated framework topology, determine how many boron atoms are contained in one conventional unit cell. Answer format: a single integer; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: a single integer; write only the number itself, with no units, explanatory text, or equals sign.

step_1.content: Represent the COF-1 topology as a periodic boroxine-linked network and identify the repeating motif.
step_1.key_point: Count boron atoms from one repeatable conventional cell only.

step_3.content: Select a conventional cell that captures the repeating arrangement without overlap ambiguity.
step_3.key_point: Use a single explicit cell boundary for counting.

step_5.content: Inside the selected cell, the highlighted boron sites sum to six effective boron atoms.
step_5.key_point: Total boron count in one conventional unit cell equals six.
